title: Texture Maps and Normal Maps

video.note: Covers importing texture maps, Base Color maps, Roughness maps, Normal maps, sRGB settings, mask/non-color texture settings, normal map compression, material connections, Texture Coordinate, Multiply, tiling/scale, and optional color tint. Ends before material instances.

learningTarget: I can import texture maps and connect base color, roughness, and normal maps correctly to build a more believable material.

bellRinger.prompt: What is the difference between color detail and surface depth detail?

vocabulary.definition: An image file used by a material to control part of the surface appearance.
A color texture that provides the main visible color detail of a material.
A grayscale texture that controls which parts of a surface look shiny or rough.
A texture that fakes small bumps and surface direction changes so lighting reacts with more detail.
A color interpretation setting that should be on for color textures and off for non-color data maps.
A non-color texture that stores control information, often in black, white, or grayscale values.
An import setting that tells Unreal how to treat and optimize a texture file.
A node that controls how texture UVs are read by a material.
The 2D coordinate layout used to place a texture on a 3D surface.
How often a texture repeats across a surface.

slides.title: Lesson 08 - Texture Maps and Normal Maps

assignment.title: Texture Map Material Build
evidenceRequired: Screenshot of material applied in the level.
Short reflection explaining what the normal map adds.

exitTicket: Why should sRGB usually be turned off for roughness maps and normal maps?
